preSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — event-driven architecture with pluggable adapters","detail":"## What was done
1. **Architecture decision**: Event-driven (EventBus) exclusively — no direct service calls for notifications
2. **Core types** in `src/models/notification.ts`: NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, Notification
3. **Adapter interface** in `src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts`: NotificationAdapter with `channel` and `send()` contract
4. **Email adapter** in `src/adapters/email.adapter.ts`: EmailNotificationAdapter (stub impl, real interface)
5. **Refactored NotificationService** to use EventBus exclusively + route through adapters
6. **Migrated OrderService** status changes from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
7. All tests pass (72/72), TypeScript compiles clean

## Constraints for other developers
- All notifications MUST flow through EventBus.emit() — never direct service calls
- All delivery channels MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface
- Adapters are registered with NotificationService via constructor injection
- Event payloads must carry all data handlers need (no additional lookups)
- To add a new channel: (1) add to NotificationChannel type, (2) implement NotificationAdapter, (3) register with NotificationService","tags":["architecture","notification","event-driven","adapters"],"scope":"src/","agent_id":"agent-1","id":"01KMYVYX08K6XW9DQV9HWWZQ1K","timestamp":"2026-03-30T07:57:30.504Z"}

{"entry_type":"decision","summary":"User notification preferences stored per-user with per-category email opt-out and frequency control, checked by NotificationService before sending","detail":"Preferences are stored per-user via the existing repository pattern (Database + BaseRepository). The model captures: global email enabled/disabled, per-notification-type opt-in/opt-out, and email frequency (instant/daily_digest/weekly_digest). NotificationService receives a PreferencesService via constructor injection and checks preferences before sending — this keeps the preference logic centralized in PreferencesService while the NotificationService just consults it. {"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 3: User notification preferences complete — model, repository, service, NotificationService integration, 15 tests","detail":"## What was done
1. **Model** (`src/models/user-preferences.ts`): UserPreferences, CategoryPreference, EmailFrequency types. Includes buildDefaultPreferences() helper and ALL_NOTIFICATION_TYPES constant.
2. **Repository** (`src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts`): PreferencesRepository extending BaseRepository with findByUserId().
3. **Service** (`src/services/preferences.service.ts`): PreferencesService with getPreferences(), updatePreferences(), shouldSendNotification(), resetPreferences(). Returns defaults for users without saved prefs.
4. **NotificationService integration** (`src/services/notification.service.ts`): Accepts optional PreferencesService via constructor. sendNotification() checks shouldSendNotification() before sending; returns null when suppressed. Fully backward-compatible — no PreferencesService means no filtering.
5. **Tests** (`tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts`): 15 tests covering defaults, updates, category opt-out, global disable, reset, and shouldSendNotification logic.

## Key design choices
- Preferences are opt-in to persist: defaults are returned in-memory until a user explicitly updates, at which point they're saved to the repository
- sendNotification() returns `Notification | null` — null means suppressed by preferences
- EmailFrequency includes daily_digest/weekly_digest for forward-compat, but only instant is functional
- All 105 tests pass, TypeScript compiles clean

## For other agents
- To check if a notification will be sent: call PreferencesService.shouldSendNotification(userId, type)
- To add new notification types: add to NotificationType in notification.ts AND to ALL_NOTIFICATION_TYPES in user-preferences.ts
- The NotificationService constructor now has an optional third parameter for PreferencesService","tags":["notification","preferences","email"],"scope":"src/","agent_id":"agent-3","id":"01KMYW9101515V8GND45FMCWEK","timestamp":"2026-03-30T08:03:02.273Z"}

postSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing
